task_completion.description: Create a second Availability Zone (AZ-b) with mirrored public and private subnets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new large container or boundary box labeled 'AZ-b' (or similar) containing smaller boxes for 'Public Subnet' and 'Private Subnet', mirroring the structure of the original AZ.

task_completion.description: Add an Application Load Balancer (ALB) and an Auto Scaling Group (ASG) spanning both AZs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Application Load Balancer icon/label connected to or spanning both public subnets, and an Auto Scaling Group boundary/icon that encompasses resources across both Availability Zones.

task_completion.description: Add Route 53 pointing to a CloudFront distribution in front of the ALB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Route 53 icon with a connection (edge/arrow) pointing to a CloudFront distribution icon, which in turn is positioned in front of or connected to the Application Load Balancer.

task_completion.description: Add NAT Gateways in public subnets and an ElastiCache Redis node in the private subnet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: NAT Gateway icons placed inside the public subnets, and an ElastiCache (or Redis) icon placed inside a private subnet.

task_completion.description: Upgrade the RDS instance to Multi-AZ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An RDS database icon that is either explicitly labeled 'Multi-AZ', duplicated across the private subnets of both AZs, or spanning across them.

task_completion.description: Export the final diagram as a PDF to '~/Diagrams/startup_aws_arch.pdf'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Draw.io 'Export as PDF' dialog being used, or a file manager/terminal window showing the newly created 'startup_aws_arch.pdf' file in the '~/Diagrams/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Draw.io graphical interface to modify the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Draw.io application open with the agent actively dragging, dropping, and connecting shapes, rather than modifying the .drawio XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The PDF was exported using the application's native export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Draw.io File -> Export as -> PDF menu or dialog should be visible in at least one screenshot, rather than the agent using a CLI conversion tool or simply renaming the file extension.

integrity.description: The agent actually created an architecture diagram rather than just pasting text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The canvas should contain standard AWS architecture icons, shapes, and connecting lines, not just a single text box containing the raw requirements from the findings text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial diagram starts with 10 shapes and 3 edges. A successful final diagram must contain at least 18 shapes and 10 edges. The verifier specifically checks the diagram's internal text for the strings 'application load balancer' and 'distribution' (referring to CloudFront). The final files must be located exactly at /home/ga/Diagrams/startup_aws_arch.drawio and /home/ga/Diagrams/startup_aws_arch.pdf.
